text:
In the small town of Willowbrook, there lived an adventurous boy named Danny. Every sunny day after school, Danny would ride his bike along the winding paths through the forest behind his house, his ears tuned to the symphony of butterflies and birds weaving through the leaves.

One autumn day, the leaves became an explosion of fiery reds and oranges as they fell. Determined to catch one, Danny ventured deeper into the woods, finding an old stone circle lined with fallen trees. It was there, near the circle's center, that he saw it—a bright orange leaf dangling just within the circle. Staring at it, he imagined himself riding through enchanted forests; his heart flared at the magical adventure.

He reached out his hand and plucked the leaf, giggling as he stared at its luminous surface. Right then, a slight breeze picked up, carrying sweet melody to his ears. It sang, "What's this leaf got?"

Danny turned quickly. "A leaf?" He frowned
